_config_note: MOBILE Lighthouse gate. Runs against the deployed Vercel PREVIEW for the PR commit (URLs are injected by .github/workflows/lighthouse.yml via the action's `urls` input - there is deliberately no `collect.url` or `startServerCommand` here, because the gate no longer measures localhost). The desktop counterpart is lighthouserc.desktop.json; both share this assertion matrix, differing only in form factor and the evidence-calibrated performance floor. Floors are documented in docs/perf/v2/gate-to-law.md and the remaining gap to the 0.95 law is tracked in Issue #42 (Vercel image-optimiser cold-start). Never lower a threshold in this file.

collect._numberOfRuns_note: CLAUDE.md hard rule: 'NO single-run Lighthouse measurements (median-of-5 only)'. The workflow runs an unrecorded warm-up curl pass over every URL before this step (so ISR and the next/image optimiser are warm), then this collects 3 scored runs and gates on the median. 3 dampens residual cold-start variance without blowing the workflow timeout; raise toward 5 when a longer window is justified.

settings._blockedUrlPatterns_note: The Vercel preview injects the vercel.live collaboration toolbar. It is a preview-only injection (absent in production) and its report-only-CSP console entry drops best-practices from 1.0 to ~0.96 and adds non-app JS to the perf trace. Blocking it makes the gate measure the application as production will serve it, without weakening any threshold.
settings._lcp_timing_note: Gather-window tuning carried over from the localhost gate. Pushes out gather closure so a slow-paint priority hero raster (Issue #42) has time to register an LCP event before Lighthouse stops listening. Lighthouse-default multipliers, not check downgrades.

assertMatrix._pattern_note: Default entry for every gated URL except /login and /signup (excluded via negative lookahead because their SEO category is intentionally off - see the second entry). Patterns are host-agnostic (the host is the per-PR preview domain, not localhost). assertMatrix entries are additive, so login/signup must be excluded here or the error-level SEO assertion would still fire on them.

assertMatrix._note: Login and signup are intentionally noindex/nofollow per fan-protection: auth pages should not be indexed (signed-in users are redirected away; indexing dilutes brand SEO). is-crawlable and canonical audits fail on these pages by design, so the SEO category is off here while perf/a11y/bp and all Core Web Vitals stay at error-level.
